name: Sodium bicarbonate
indications:
## Main indications

Correction of metabolic acidosis.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: 1–2 mEq/kg IV diluted in selected situations (prescription).

pediatrico.presentation: Capsules: 1000 mg; Oral solution 10% (compounded preparation): 100 mg of sodium bicarbonate/mL (1.2 mEq of bicarbonate/mL and 1.2 mEq of sodium/mL); IV solution 1 Molar: 1 mEq of bicarbonate/mL See formulation
pediatrico.infusionRate: Continuous infusion over 4 to 8 hours with infusion pump.
pediatrico.dose: Severe acute metabolic acidosis (IV): mEq required = (desired serum bicarbonate - current bicarbonate) x 0.3 x weight in kg. Chronic metabolic acidosis (PO): mEq required = (desired serum bicarbonate - current bicarbonate) x 0.6 x weight in kg. See CIME Electrolytes Bulletin.
pediatrico.notes: Always treat the cause of acidosis. Correct hypokalemia first. PO administration: administer 1-3 hours after meals; 1 g sodium bicarbonate capsules contain 12 mEq of bicarbonate and 12 mEq of sodium. IV administration: peripheral route: dilute to 1/6; central route: IV push: slow up to 1 mEq/mL, IV infusion: dilute to 0.5 mEq/mL in 5% dextrose and infuse over more than 2 h. Incompatible with calcium, magnesium, and atropine

neonatal.dose: 1–2 mEq/kg in cardiac arrest with acidosis per NRP.

stability:
## Pediatric guide

- Keep at room temperature. Discard remainder.

## General

- Do not mix with calcium in the same line.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

PO: gastric distension and flatulence. IV: tissue necrosis (avoid extravasation), hyponatremia, metabolic alkalosis, hypocalcemia.
